title: Carbon-Free Hydrogen: Publication of the Specifications of the First Call for Tenders in France
content: The French government has published the official specifications (â€œcahier des chargesâ€) for a first competitive tender designed to support the production of decarbonized hydrogen by electrolysis in France. This initiative is part of Franceâ€™s National Hydrogen Strategy, which was updated in April 2025 to reinforce ambitions for a low-carbon hydrogen industry. The tender aims to help develop around 1 GW of electrolyser capacity in France over the coming years. It supports hydrogen production destined mainly for industrial uses (excluding refining). The scheme provides state financial aid proportional to the amount of hydrogen produced, helping hydrogen made from renewable or low-carbon sources become competitive with fossil hydrogen.
